rules.name: read-readmes-before-edit
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., packages/*, crates/*), read all README.md files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: read-readmes-before-write
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., packages/*, crates/*), read all README.md files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: start-dev-watch-before-editing-source
rules.evidence: If you are changing Next.js source or integration tests, start `pnpm --filter=next dev` in a separate terminal session before making edits (unless it is already running).

rules.name: bootstrap-build-after-checkout
rules.evidence: Always run a full bootstrap build after switching branches:

rules.name: use-new-test-for-new-test-files
rules.evidence: Generating tests using `pnpm new-test` is mandatory.

rules.name: no-settimeout-in-tests
rules.evidence: Use `retry()` from `next-test-utils` instead of `setTimeout` for waiting

rules.name: no-check-helper-in-tests
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: prefer-fixture-dirs-over-inline-files
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects

rules.name: pr-description-llm-marker
rules.evidence: When writing PR descriptions, you MUST include the following HTML comment at the bottom of the description:

rules.name: no-ai-attribution-commit
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: no-ai-attribution-pr
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: no-gh-pr-ready
rules.evidence: Do NOT mark PRs as "ready for review" (`gh pr ready`) - leave PRs in draft mode and let the user decide when to mark them ready

rules.name: pre-validate-before-commit
rules.evidence: Pre-validate before committing to avoid slow lint-staged failures (~2 min each):

rules.name: rebuild-before-mode-specific-tests
rules.evidence: When running Next.js integration tests, you must rebuild if source files have changed:

rules.name: no-literal-secrets-in-commands
rules.evidence: Mirror CI env **names and modes** exactly, but do not inline literal secret values in commands.

rules.name: no-commit-env-secret-files
rules.evidence: Never commit local secret files; if documenting env setup, use placeholder-only examples.

rules.name: no-remote-url-mutation-ssh
rules.evidence: Do not switch remotes to HTTPS, mutate remote URLs, retry repeatedly, or attempt another authentication workaround unless the user explicitly requests it.

rules.name: ssh-preflight-before-force-push-rebase
rules.evidence: Before a force-push or stack rebase that may hydrate partial-clone objects, prefer a lightweight SSH preflight.

rules.name: new-flags-config-shared-wiring
rules.evidence: New flags: add type in `config-shared.ts`, schema in `config-schema.ts`, and `define-env.ts` when used in user-bundled code.

rules.name: define-env-edit-guardrails
rules.evidence: `define-env.ts` affects user bundling; it does not control pre-compiled runtime bundle internals.

rules.name: dce-require-compile-time-branch
rules.evidence: Keep `require()` behind compile-time `if/else` branches for DCE (avoid early-return/throw patterns).

rules.name: react-server-dom-webpack-entry-base-only
rules.evidence: `react-server-dom-webpack/*` imports must stay in `entry-base.ts`; consume via component module exports elsewhere.

rules.name: snapshot-update-match-ci-env
rules.evidence: When updating snapshots, always run the test with the exact env flags the CI job uses (check `.github/workflows/build_and_test.yml` `afterBuild:` sections).

rules.name: app-page-no-relative-require
rules.evidence: You cannot require internal modules with relative paths because they won't be resolvable from the user's project.

rules.name: no-browser-back-in-act-tests
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: avoid-new-super-imports-rust
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

rules.name: careful-highlight-line-numbers-docs
rules.evidence: When adding `highlight={...}` attributes to code blocks, carefully count the actual line numbers within the code block

rules.name: flag-nonstandard-header-reads
rules.evidence: When reviewing PRs: if new code reads a request header that is not a standard HTTP header (like `content-type`, `accept`, `user-agent`, `host`, `authorization`, `cookie`, etc.), flag it for security review.

uncompilable: Kill the background watch process when done iterating — cleanup reminder with no specific tool-call trigger.
Prefer `pnpm --filter=next types` over a full build when only checking type errors — context-dependent preference, not a fixed action trigger.

Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters; capture output once and analyze from file (repeated under Context-Efficient Workflows) — stateful/temporal guidance across multiple calls, not a single-call trigger.
Run the pr-status script when the user asks about CI failures/PR reviews/status — triggered by user intent, not a tool call.
Prioritize blocking failures first: build, lint, types, then tests (repeated under CI Analysis Tips) — general prioritization judgment, not action-bindable.
Assume failures are real until disproven; treat 'Known Flaky Tests' as context, not auto-dismissal — general judgment call.
For module-resolution/build-graph fixes, use the normal mode-specific test command (repeated under Development Anti-Patterns) — advisory test-selection judgment, not a fixed trigger.
Prioritize CI failures over review comments — general prioritization, not action-bindable.
Common fast-check recipes (rust check/build → cargo fmt; lint/build → prettier --write) — conditioned on observing a specific CI failure type, not a static command trigger.
Use `DEBUG=next:*` for debug logging / use `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` when testing locally — soft dev tips, not obligations tied to an action.
Never print or paste secret values in chat responses, commits, or shared logs — not bindable to a specific tool call (chat responses aren't a tool).
If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials — judgment call on missing state, not a static trigger.
When sharing command output, summarize and redact sensitive-looking values — about how output is presented, not a tool-call trigger.

Never re-read the same section of a file without code changes; for generated files (dist/, node_modules/, .next/) search only, don't read — concerns the Read tool, outside the Bash/Edit/Write trigger scope.
Don't re-run the same build/test command without code changes; re-analyze saved output instead — stateful across calls, not a single-call trigger.
Group related edits across files, then run one build, not build-per-edit — workflow-level judgment, not one action.
Don't re-fetch the same API data to analyze from different angles — stateful across multiple calls.
Keep commit messages concise and descriptive — general style/tone.
PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why — general style/tone.

Split work into smaller, individually verifiable tasks; verify each task before moving on; choose the right verification method; ask the user when unclear how to verify — general task-management judgment, not tied to one tool call.
Cache components enables PPR by default; test PPR via __NEXT_CACHE_COMPONENTS=true rather than dedicated ppr suites — contextual test-strategy knowledge, not a fixed action trigger.
Quick smoke testing with toy apps for fast feedback — optional workflow tip.
Mode-specific tests need skipStart: true + manual next.start() in beforeAll — describes a correct pattern, not phrasable as a reliable positive regex trigger.
Don't rely on exact log messages; filter by content patterns, find sequences not positions — general test-writing style.
Always match exact CI env vars when reproducing CI failures locally — too broad/contextual to bind without firing on every routine test run.
Set __NEXT_SHOW_IGNORE_LISTED=true to see full stack traces — informational tip, not an obligation.
cargo fmt uses ASCII order (uppercase before lowercase) — informational fact, not an obligation on a specific action.
Internal compiler error (ICE)? Delete incremental compilation artifacts and retry — conditioned on an error state, not a static command pattern.
If Turbopack produces unexpected errors after switching branches or pulling, check for a stale native binary and delete it — conditioned on a symptom, not a static trigger.
